name: Delftware Tulip Vase
primary_sources: Caiger-Smith, Alan (1973). Tin-Glaze Pottery in Europe and the Islamic World: The Tradition of 1000 Years in Maiolica, Faience and Delftware. Faber and Faber. ISBN 0-571-09349-3.

visual_attributes.morphology: tall, multi-tiered vase with bulbous sections and narrow necks
distinctive_features: blue and white floral and geometric patterns
multiple spouts for holding individual tulips
ornate decorative motifs inspired by Chinese and Japanese designs
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: tiered structure with multiple openings for flowers

domain_specific_visual.TIN_GLAZE: white tin glaze with cobalt blue underglaze
domain_specific_visual.DECORATIVE_STYLE: influenced by Chinese porcelain and Japanese Imari ware

domain_specific_functional.SYMBOLIC_ROLE: represents the Dutch Golden Age and trade connections
domain_specific_functional.CULTURAL_IMPORTANCE: iconic example of Dutch craftsmanship and artistry

relationships.description: One of the main factories producing Delftware Tulip Vases
Designs and techniques influenced by imported Chinese porcelain

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: primarily the Netherlands, exported across Europe
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the height of Dutch artistic achievement and international trade
primary_sources: Caiger-Smith, Alan (1973). Tin-Glaze Pottery in Europe and the Islamic World: The Tradition of 1000 Years in Maiolica, Faience and Delftware. Faber and Faber. ISBN 0-571-09349-3.

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic blue and white vase
NOT lacking multiple spouts for tulips
NOT a modern or minimalist design
